name: Sheresh Communes

street_level_faction_presence.street_signals: Symbols, uniform cues, work marks, and rumor shorthand make power visible in daily movement.

greetings: Survival-aware greetings often include weather, dome status, or route condition before personal matters.
hospitality_rules: Warmth, shelter, and containment courtesy define good hospitality more than abundance.
taboo_topics: mocking mutation scars or dome necessity
class_expectations: researchers, wardens, and laborers alike are judged by steadiness, competence, and respect for survival procedure
faith_etiquette: aurora wonder and communal endurance create solemn but not joyless ritual conduct
suspicious_behavior: moving between communes with unexplained residue or devices
outsider_mistakes: confusing reserved behavior for coldness rather than resource discipline

occupation_and_daily_life.daily_rhythm: Heat management, repair, research, route timing, preserved food routines, and aurora or containment watch shape daily existence.
occupation_and_daily_life.work_rhythm_by_calendar_or_season: Axial flips, aurora conditions, and freeze severity reorganize travel, experimentation, and public gathering.
occupation_and_daily_life.ordinary_texture: Air smells of ozone, lamp oil, snow-wet cloth, boiled broth, resin, and metal warmed too near heat sources. Sounds are quieter and more careful than in temperate polities.

travel_and_transition_texture: Travel between communes requires escorts, weather judgment, shelter knowledge, and respect for handoff custom at safe points.
dwelling_and_settlement_life: Communal heat spaces, compact sleeping arrangements, layered fabric barriers, and emergency stores define homes across class lines.
relationship_and_reputation_logic: People are remembered for whether they hold steady in crisis and whether they endanger others through negligence.
region_specific_emotional_tone: Sheresh stories are intimate, survival-bound, and often luminous with contained dread: scarce warmth, scientific wonder, quiet solidarity, and the fear of failure in hard places.
